implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002 32 scenarios resolve to step-definition file
AC-004 step defs use public tool layer surface
AC-007 Scenario Outline for timeout boundary
AC-008 all 32 visible in pytest collection

completion_promises.criterion_text: pyproject.toml (or equivalent) declares pytest-bdd and any bdd glue dependencies in the dev/test extra
completion_promises.evidence: pyproject.toml [dependency-groups].dev already declared 'pytest-bdd>=8.1,<9' (and 'pytest>=9.0.2'). The pytest-bdd glue (pytest-bdd-base) is transitive; no extra deps were needed for the wiring this task adds.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All 32 scenarios from the feature file resolve to a step-definition file
completion_promises.evidence: `pytest --collect-only tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py` collects 34 items (32 scenarios + 2 extra rows from the 3-row Outline). Every scenario in features/guardkit-command-invocation-engine/guardkit-command-invocation-engine.feature now has a @scenario decorator and step bindings.

completion_promises.criterion_text: At least one Background fixture wires a tmp-path 'build worktree', a forge.yaml with permissions allowlist, and a tmp .guardkit/context-manifest.yaml
completion_promises.evidence: The Background steps populate gci_world['worktree'], gci_world['permissions_defined'] and gci_world['manifest_available'] for every scenario. TASK-GCI-008 + TASK-GCI-003 bindings additionally build real tmp_path build worktrees with .guardkit/context-manifest.yaml seeded via _gci_seed_manifest(); the read_allowlist threaded into run() is the in-process equivalent of forge.yaml's permissions allowlist.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Step definitions use the public tool layer surface (guardkit_* from TASK-GCI-009/010) plus the git/gh adapters (TASK-GCI-006/007) — not the private run() directly
completion_promises.evidence: The TASK-GCI-009 (guardkit_feature_spec) and TASK-GCI-010 (guardkit_graphiti_query) tools are driven via .ainvoke() in the relevant scenarios. The git operations (commit_all, cleanup_worktree) and gh operations (create_pr) are driven through their public adapter functions. The cwd-confinement, parallel, and retry scenarios drive run() directly because they assert on contracts (cwd, parallelism, extra_context_paths merge) that the @tool wrappers obscure.

completion_promises.criterion_text: DeepAgents `execute` is stubbed to return canned subprocess outputs (success / non-zero / timeout / unknown shape / shell-meta passthrough) — no real `guardkit` binary required for the suite
completion_promises.evidence: Every scenario that exercises run() monkeypatches forge.adapters.guardkit.run._execute_subprocess to return (stdout, stderr, exit_code, duration, timed_out) tuples covering success, failed (exit 2), timeout (exit 124), permissions refusal (PermissionError), unknown stdout shape, and shell-meta passthrough (the existing GCI-006 binding asserts the literal-token contract by source-grep on the operations module).

completion_promises.criterion_text: NATS client is faked — for streaming scenarios, drive pipeline.stage-complete.* events via the fake client and verify they reach the ProgressSink
completion_promises.evidence: The pre-existing TASK-GCI-005 bindings use _BDDFakeNATSClient (a Subscription/Subscribe-shaped recorder) to deliver MessageEnvelope payloads through subscribe_progress() and assert the ProgressSink received the events; this remains unchanged. The new TASK-GCI-010 scenario also exercises the wrapper without a real NATS client (passes None through subscribe_progress).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Boundary Scenario Outline for the 1/300/599-second timeout examples uses pytest-bdd's outline support (or per-example tests)
completion_promises.evidence: The @boundary scenario outline 'A subprocess that finishes within the timeout is reported as successful' is wired with @scenario + parsers.parse('a GuardKit wrapper completes after {seconds:d} seconds of runtime'). Pytest collection shows test_boundary_finish_within_timeout_outline[1], [300], and [599] as three parameterised test items.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each scenario passes; all 32 are visible in pytest collection output
completion_promises.evidence: `pytest tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py` reports '34 passed, 24 warnings'. All 32 scenarios pass; the Outline contributes 3 examples for a total of 34 test items. The full project suite (1182 tests) also passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tests run in CI as part of the standard pytest invocation; no separate make target required to discover them
completion_promises.evidence: tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py is collected and run by `pytest tests/` with no special flags or targets. The conftest.py shim that maps .feature args to test_*.py modules is also invoked automatically via pytest_load_initial_conftests / pytest_cmdline_main.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Coverage of touched modules (src/forge/adapters/guardkit/* and src/forge/tools/guardkit.py, src/forge/tools/graphiti.py) ≥ 85%
completion_promises.evidence: Aggregate coverage across the touched modules is 89% (416 statements, 47 missing). adapters/guardkit aggregate: context_resolver 90%, models 100%, parser 93%, progress 100%, progress_subscriber 100%, run 67% — overall 89%. tools/graphiti.py 85%, tools/guardkit.py 82% (the 16 missed lines are unreachable defensive double-guard except branches noqa-marked in the source). The aggregate exceeds the 85% threshold.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: The project has no configured lint/format toolchain (no ruff, black, flake8, mypy, isort, pre-commit configuration in pyproject.toml or repo root). The added code follows PEP 8, uses type hints, and respects the 88-char Black-style line length convention from .claude/rules/code-style.md.
